architecture.description:
### High Level Architecture

#### Application type
Serverless event-driven autonomous coding agent platform deployed on AWS (API + orchestrator + isolated compute)

#### Layers
- Input layer: REST API (API Gateway + Cognito + WAFv2), Slack integration, Linear integration, Webhook (HMAC)
- Orchestration layer: Lambda Durable Functions driving task state machine (admission, hydration, session mgmt, finalization)
- Compute layer: Isolated MicroVM sessions (Bedrock AgentCore Runtime / optional ECS Fargate) running Python agent with Claude Agent SDK
- Data layer: DynamoDB tables (tasks, events, approvals, concurrency, repos, webhooks), S3 (attachments, traces), Secrets Manager, AgentCore Memory
- Integration layer: GitHub (clone/push/PR via PAT), Amazon Bedrock (LLM inference), DNS Firewall (egress control)

#### Boundaries
- Internet to API Gateway (WAFv2 protected, Cognito JWT or HMAC auth)
- API Gateway Lambda handlers to Orchestrator Lambda (async invoke)
- Orchestrator to AgentCore Runtime (SigV4 invoke_agent_runtime across VPC boundary)
- AgentCore MicroVM to external services (NAT Gateway, DNS Firewall allowlist: GitHub, npm, PyPI, AWS)
- Agent to Bedrock Runtime (VPC endpoint, model invocation for code generation)

#### Technology components
- AWS CDK (TypeScript) - Infrastructure as Code, single stack deployment
- Python 3.x + Claude Agent SDK + FastAPI - Agent runtime in MicroVM container
- TypeScript + Node.js 24 - Lambda handlers, orchestrator, CLI
- Amazon Bedrock (Claude Sonnet 4/Haiku/Opus) - LLM inference via cross-region profiles
- DynamoDB - Task state, events, approvals, concurrency, repo config, webhooks
- Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime - MicroVM isolation, session lifecycle (Firecracker)
- Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Memory - Cross-session semantic/episodic memory
- Cedar policy engine (WASM Lambda layer) - Tool-call access control and HITL gates

#### Deployment architecture
- Single AWS account, single CDK stack (backgroundagent-dev)
- VPC with private subnets (AgentCore Runtime), public subnets (NAT Gateway)
- VPC Endpoints: S3, DynamoDB, ECR, CloudWatch, Secrets Manager, Bedrock Runtime, STS
- DNS Firewall with domain allowlist (observation mode default)
- Single NAT Gateway for internet egress (configurable multi-AZ)

#### APIs

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| /v1/tasks | POST | Create coding task (new_task, pr_iteration, pr_review) |
| /v1/tasks | GET | List user tasks (paginated, filterable) |
| /v1/tasks/{id} | GET | Get task details with error classification |
| /v1/tasks/{id} | DELETE | Cancel task (stops session, releases concurrency) |
| /v1/tasks/{id}/events | GET | Task audit trail (state transitions, events) |
| /v1/tasks/{id}/approve | POST | Approve Cedar HITL gate |
| /v1/tasks/{id}/deny | POST | Deny Cedar HITL gate |
| /v1/webhooks/tasks | POST | Create task via HMAC webhook |

#### Security relevant components
- Amazon Cognito User Pool (JWT auth, no MFA in dev) + HMAC-SHA256 webhooks
- Bedrock Guardrails (PROMPT_ATTACK filter, fail-closed input screening at submission and hydration)
- Cedar policy engine (PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks, hard/soft deny, HITL approval gates)
- MicroVM session isolation (Firecracker, per-task compute/memory/filesystem)
- DNS Firewall domain allowlisting + Security Group TCP 443 only egress
- Output secret scanner (PostToolUse redaction of AWS keys, tokens, private keys)
- WAFv2 (CRS, known bad inputs, IP rate limit 1000/5min)

### Low Level Architecture
- API Gateway REST API (v1 stage) with Cognito authorizer and REQUEST authorizer for webhooks
- WAFv2 Web ACL with AWS Managed Rules (CRS, KnownBadInputs) and IP rate limiting
- Cognito User Pool (email sign-in, SRP/password auth flows, no MFA)
- Lambda handlers: CreateTask, GetTask, ListTasks, CancelTask, GetTaskEvents, ApproveTask, DenyTask, GetPending, NudgeTask, ConfirmUploads, GetTraceUrl, WebhookAuthorizer, WebhookCreateTask, GetPolicies
- TaskOrchestrator Lambda (Durable Functions, checkpoint/replay, 30s poll interval)
- AgentCore Runtime (Docker image from agent/Dockerfile, 8h max lifetime, /invocations + /ping endpoints)
- FastAPI server (port 8080) spawning background thread for pipeline execution
- Pipeline stages: setup_repo, assemble_prompt, run_agent (Claude Agent SDK), post_hooks (ensure_committed, ensure_pr, verify_build)
- DynamoDB Tables: TaskTable (GSI: UserStatus, Status, Idempotency), TaskEventsTable (DDB Streams to FanOut + ApprovalMetrics), TaskApprovalsTable, UserConcurrencyTable, RepoTable, WebhookTable, TaskNudgesTable, SlackInstallationTable, SlackUserMappingTable, LinearProjectMappingTable, LinearUserMappingTable
- S3 Buckets: AttachmentsBucket (versioned, 90-day lifecycle), TraceArtifactsBucket (7-day TTL)
- Secrets Manager: GitHub PAT, Slack signing/client secrets, webhook secrets (bgagent/webhook/*), Linear API token
- Bedrock Guardrail (InputGuardrail, PROMPT_ATTACK MEDIUM strength)
- Cedar WASM Lambda Layer for policy evaluation
- AgentCore Memory (semantic knowledge + episodic strategies, per-repo namespaces)
- SlackIntegration (commands, events, interactions, OAuth, notify via DDB Streams fan-out)
- LinearIntegration (webhook processor, project/user mapping tables)
- ConcurrencyReconciler (15-min schedule, drift correction)
- StrandedTaskReconciler (catches orphaned SUBMITTED/HYDRATING tasks)
- PendingUploadCleanup (30-min auto-cancel for unconfirmed uploads)
- TaskDashboard (CloudWatch dashboard for operational monitoring)
- VPC: Private subnets + NAT Gateway + VPC Endpoints + DNS Firewall rule group

assumptions.content: The AgentCore Runtime is the primary compute backend; ECS Fargate is commented out and not actively deployed.

metadata.value: The ECS cluster construct import is commented out in agent.ts. The ECS strategy code exists but the CDK stack does not instantiate it. AgentCore is the only active compute path.

assumptions.content: DNS Firewall is deployed in observation mode (ALERT only, not BLOCK) based on default configuration.

metadata.value: The DnsFirewall construct is instantiated with observationMode: true. Non-allowlisted domains are logged but not blocked. Enforcement requires explicit operator opt-in.

assumptions.content: A single shared GitHub PAT is used for all repositories; per-repo scoped tokens are not yet implemented.

metadata.value: One Secrets Manager secret (GitHubTokenSecret) stores the PAT. The orchestrator passes it to all agent sessions regardless of repo. GitHub App integration is listed as planned in the roadmap.

assumptions.content: The orchestrator uses Lambda Durable Functions for checkpoint/replay, enabling hours-long task monitoring without compute charges.

metadata.value: Confirmed in ORCHESTRATOR.md and task-orchestrator.ts. The orchestrator polls every 30s via waitForCondition, suspending between polls. Execution duration supports up to 1 year.

assumptions.content: All DynamoDB tables use on-demand capacity mode with PITR enabled and 90-day TTL for task data.

metadata.value: SECURITY.md confirms PITR on all tables. Task retention defaults to 90 days (TASK_RETENTION_DAYS env var). On-demand mode inferred from absence of provisioned capacity in constructs.

assumptions.content: The agent container image must fit within 2GB (AgentCore hard limit) including OS, runtimes, and agent code.

metadata.value: COMPUTE.md documents the 2GB non-adjustable image limit. The Dockerfile builds from repo root to include contracts/constants.json. Repos exceeding this limit require ECS backend.

assumptions.content: Slack and Linear integrations are always deployed but require post-deploy secret population to become functional.

metadata.value: Both SlackIntegration and LinearIntegration constructs are unconditionally instantiated. Secrets (signing secret, client secret, API tokens) are created empty and populated after deployment.

assumptions.content: The TaskEventsTable DynamoDB Stream has exactly two consumers (FanOutConsumer and ApprovalMetricsPublisher); adding a third requires migration to Kinesis Data Streams.

metadata.value: Noted in the CDK stack comments and task-events-table.ts. DynamoDB Streams supports max 2 Lambda consumers per stream. A third consumer would require Kinesis Data Streams for DynamoDB.
